Category: Media & Entertainment: Publishing, Film, Advertising & Communications, Gaming
Who is this for: Community managers, game developers, content moderators, customer support representatives, product managers, trust and safety specialists, digital marketing managers
Team or Dept this is for: Product Development, Customer Support, Trust and Safety, Community Management, Legal, Marketing
Description: False positives, missed abuse, and community trust.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with online gaming platforms and a general awareness of how online communities function is all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the most common causes of false positives and missed abuse in AI moderation systems, and explain how these errors affect player experience and community trust. You will also apply practical strategies to evaluate and improve AI moderation tools, helping you build safer and more welcoming online gaming environments.
